rationale: The two papers are in very different substantive domains (credit-card repayment defaults vs. advertising disclosure herding), which suggests the citation may be peripheral. However, the citing paper uses a peer-based IV design, and the cited paper’s core contribution is a peer-effect identification strategy with instruments, so a methodological-use link is plausible. Because the evidence includes only a DOI match with no citation-context text, it is not safe to drop as obvious mention-only.
